<!-- Your title above should be a short description of what
was changed. Do not include the issue number in the title. -->

#### References to other Issues or PRs
<!-- If this pull request fixes an issue, write "Fixes #NNNN" in that exact
format, e.g. "Fixes #1234" (see
https://tinyurl.com/auto-closing for more information). Also, please
write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is
open. -->
I have not found any pull requests related to my proposed changes.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, and the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation have been added throughout the entire file. No other code was modified or deleted.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

#### Release Notes

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/pulls/comments/967557901","pull_request_review_id":1103019821,"id":967557901,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAAnGFs45q8MN","diff_hunk":"@@ -754,6 +762,158 @@ def gaunt(l_1, l_2, l_3, m_1, m_2, m_3, prec=None):
 
 
 
+def real_gaunt(l_1, l_2, l_3, m_1, m_2, m_3, prec=None):
+    r"""
+    Calculate the real Gaunt coefficient.
+
+    Explanation
+    ===========
+
+    The real Gaunt coefficient is defined as the integral over three
+    real spherical harmonics:
+
+    .. math::
+
+        \begin{aligned}
+        \operatorname{RealGaunt}(l_1,l_2,l_3,m_1,m_2,m_3)
+        &=\int Z^{m_1}_{l_1}(\Omega)
+         Z^{m_2}_{l_2}(\Omega) Z^{m_3}_{l_3}(\Omega) \,d\Omega \\
+        \end{aligned}
+        
+    Alternatively, it can be defined in terms of the standard Gaunt
+    coefficient by relating the real spherical harmonics to the standard
+    spherical harmonics via a unitary transformation `U`, i.e. 
+    `Z^{m}_{l}(\Omega)=\sum_{m'}U^{m}_{m'}Y^{m'}_{l}(\Omega)` [Homeier96]_.
+    The real Gaunt coefficient is then defined as
+    
+    .. math::
+
+        \begin{aligned}
+        \operatorname{RealGaunt}(l_1,l_2,l_3,m_1,m_2,m_3)
+        &=\int Z^{m_1}_{l_1}(\Omega)
+         Z^{m_2}_{l_2}(\Omega) Z^{m_3}_{l_3}(\Omega) \,d\Omega \\
+        &=\sum_{m'_1 m'_2 m'_3} U^{m_1}_{m'_1}U^{m_2}_{m'_2}U^{m_3}_{m'_3}
+         \operatorname{Gaunt}(l_1,l_2,l_3,m'_1,m'_2,m'_3)
+        \end{aligned}
+    
+    The unitary matrix `U` has components
+    
+    ..math::
+    
+        \begin{aligned}
+        U^m_{m'} = \delta_{|m||m'|}*(\delta_{m'0}\delta_{m0} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\big[\Theta(m)
+        \big(\delta_{m'm}+(-1)^{m'}\delta_{m'-m}\big)+i\Theta(-m)\big((-1)^{-m}
+        \delta_{m'-m}-\delta_{m'm}*(-1)^{m'-m}\big)\big])
+        \end{aligned}
+        
+    where `\delta_{ij}` is the Kronecker delta symbol and `\Theta` is a step
+    function defined as
+    
+    ..math::
+    
+        \begin{aligned}
+        \Theta(x) = \begin{cases} 1 \,\text{for}\, x > 0 \\ 0 \,\text{for}\, x \leq 0 \end{cases}
+        \end{aligned}
+
+    Parameters
+    ==========
+
+    l_1, l_2, l_3, m_1, m_2, m_3 :
+        Integer.
+    prec - precision, default: ``None``.
+        Providing a precision can
+        drastically speed up the calculation.
+
+    Returns
+    =======
+
+    Rational number times the square root of a rational number
+    (if ``prec=None``), or real number if a precision is given.
+
+    Examples
+    ========
+
+    >>> from sympy.physics.wigner import real_gaunt
+    >>> real_gaunt(2,2,4,-1,-1,0)
+    -2/(7*sqrt(pi))
+    >>> real_gaunt(10,10,20,-9,-9,0).n(64)
+    -0.00002480019791932209313156167...
+
+    It is an error to use non-integer values for `l` and `m`::
+
+        real_gaunt(2.8,0.5,1.3,0,0,0)
+        Traceback (most recent call last):
+        ...
+        ValueError: l values must be integer
+        real_gaunt(2,2,4,0.7,1,-3.4)
+        Traceback (most recent call last):
+        ...
+        ValueError: m values must be integer
+
+    Notes
+    =====
+
+    The real Gaunt coefficient inherits from the standard Gaunt coefficient, 
+    the invariance under any permutation of the pairs `(l_i, m_i)` and the 
+    requirement that the sum of the `l_i` be even to yield a non-zero value. 
+    It also obeys the following symmetry rules:
+
+    - zero for `l_1`, `l_2`, `l_3` not fulfiling the condition 
+    `l_1 \in \{l_{\text{max}}, l_{\text{max}}-2, \ldots, l_{\text{min}}\}`,
+    where `l_{\text{max}} = l_2+l_3`, 
+    
+    ..math::
+    
+        \begin{aligned}
+        l_{\text{min}} = \begin{cases} \kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3) & \text{if}\, 
+        \kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3) + l_{\text{max}}\, \text{is even} \\ 
+        \kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3)+1 & \text{if}\, \kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3) +
+        l_{\text{max}}\, \text{is odd}\end{cases}
+        \end{aligned}
+        
+    and `\kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3) = \max{\big(|l_2-l_3|, \min{\big(|m_2+m_3|, 
+    |m_2-m_3|\big)}\big)}`
+      
+    - zero for an odd number of negative `m_i`
+
+    Algorithms
+    ==========
+
+    This function uses the algorithms of [Homeier96]_ and [Rasch03]_ to
+    calculate the value of the real Gaunt coefficient exactly. Note that
+    the formula used in [Rasch03]_ contains alternating sums over large
+    factorials and is therefore unsuitable for finite precision arithmetic
+    and only useful for a computer algebra system [Rasch03]_. However, this
+    function can in principle use any algorithm that computes the Gaunt
+    coefficient, so it is suitable for finite precision arithmetic in so far 
+    as the algorithm which computes the Gaunt coefficient is.
+
+    """
+    if int(l_1) != l_1 or int(l_2) != l_2 or int(l_3) != l_3:
+        raise ValueError("l values must be integer")
+    if int(m_1) != m_1 or int(m_2) != m_2 or int(m_3) != m_3:
+        raise ValueError("m values must be integer")","path":"sympy/physics/wigner.py","position":164,"original_position":164,"commit_id":"773d9e0d43ea9384785d989c5f6e26ca904be058","original_commit_id":"773d9e0d43ea9384785d989c5f6e26ca904be058","user":{"login":"AntiPhoton47","id":112662371,"node_id":"U_kgDOBrcXYw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/112662371?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47","html_url":"https://github.com/AntiPhoton47","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/AntiPhoton47/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Hi Christopher,

my apologies for not responding, I was wrapped up in something else and forgot to reply to you. I really appreciate your code suggestions as you're right, they do make the program more concise and reduce the runtime substantially. I will implement your suggestions momentarily. I think we should leave the 'ugnt' variable as a scalar initially because I am concerned about memory usage (for large l values).

Real spherical harmonics are used a lot in quantum chemistry calculations, and I used them all throughout my master's research, so I think having a real gaunt function would be quite useful to others as well. I can solicit some other opinions however. 

I am just running some tests now but I have tested this algorithm in the past very extensively for my research. I have included some information about the real gaunt coefficient in the comments above the code. For reference, the l values can only be natural numbers. 

I mean symbolic in the same sense as the regular Gaunt algorithm, which only accepts integer arguments. I would wager that the symbolic case is not of interest to anyone, so I say we leave it out until people request it. What do you I have not found any pull requests related to my proposed changes.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, and the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation have been added throughout the entire file. No other code was modified or deleted.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Thanks to Christopher Smith for saving some space by redefining the step function into a lambda function.

I have not found any pull requests related to my proposed changes.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, and the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation have been added throughout the entire file. No other code was modified or deleted.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Thanks to Christopher Smith for saving some space by redefining the step function into a lambda function.

I have not found any pull requests related to my proposed changes.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, and the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation have been added throughout the entire file. No other code was modified or deleted.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Big thanks to Christopher Smith for suggesting a number of modifications that have made the program more concise and reduced the runtime significantly.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/pulls/comments/967772450","pull_request_review_id":1103250461,"id":967772450,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAAnGFs45rwki","diff_hunk":"@@ -754,6 +762,158 @@ def gaunt(l_1, l_2, l_3, m_1, m_2, m_3, prec=None):
 
 
 
+def real_gaunt(l_1, l_2, l_3, m_1, m_2, m_3, prec=None):
+    r"""
+    Calculate the real Gaunt coefficient.
+
+    Explanation
+    ===========
+
+    The real Gaunt coefficient is defined as the integral over three
+    real spherical harmonics:
+
+    .. math::
+
+        \begin{aligned}
+        \operatorname{RealGaunt}(l_1,l_2,l_3,m_1,m_2,m_3)
+        &=\int Z^{m_1}_{l_1}(\Omega)
+         Z^{m_2}_{l_2}(\Omega) Z^{m_3}_{l_3}(\Omega) \,d\Omega \\
+        \end{aligned}
+        
+    Alternatively, it can be defined in terms of the standard Gaunt
+    coefficient by relating the real spherical harmonics to the standard
+    spherical harmonics via a unitary transformation `U`, i.e. 
+    `Z^{m}_{l}(\Omega)=\sum_{m'}U^{m}_{m'}Y^{m'}_{l}(\Omega)` [Homeier96]_.
+    The real Gaunt coefficient is then defined as
+    
+    .. math::
+
+        \begin{aligned}
+        \operatorname{RealGaunt}(l_1,l_2,l_3,m_1,m_2,m_3)
+        &=\int Z^{m_1}_{l_1}(\Omega)
+         Z^{m_2}_{l_2}(\Omega) Z^{m_3}_{l_3}(\Omega) \,d\Omega \\
+        &=\sum_{m'_1 m'_2 m'_3} U^{m_1}_{m'_1}U^{m_2}_{m'_2}U^{m_3}_{m'_3}
+         \operatorname{Gaunt}(l_1,l_2,l_3,m'_1,m'_2,m'_3)
+        \end{aligned}
+    
+    The unitary matrix `U` has components
+    
+    ..math::
+    
+        \begin{aligned}
+        U^m_{m'} = \delta_{|m||m'|}*(\delta_{m'0}\delta_{m0} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\big[\Theta(m)
+        \big(\delta_{m'm}+(-1)^{m'}\delta_{m'-m}\big)+i\Theta(-m)\big((-1)^{-m}
+        \delta_{m'-m}-\delta_{m'm}*(-1)^{m'-m}\big)\big])
+        \end{aligned}
+        
+    where `\delta_{ij}` is the Kronecker delta symbol and `\Theta` is a step
+    function defined as
+    
+    ..math::
+    
+        \begin{aligned}
+        \Theta(x) = \begin{cases} 1 \,\text{for}\, x > 0 \\ 0 \,\text{for}\, x \leq 0 \end{cases}
+        \end{aligned}
+
+    Parameters
+    ==========
+
+    l_1, l_2, l_3, m_1, m_2, m_3 :
+        Integer.
+    prec - precision, default: ``None``.
+        Providing a precision can
+        drastically speed up the calculation.
+
+    Returns
+    =======
+
+    Rational number times the square root of a rational number
+    (if ``prec=None``), or real number if a precision is given.
+
+    Examples
+    ========
+
+    >>> from sympy.physics.wigner import real_gaunt
+    >>> real_gaunt(2,2,4,-1,-1,0)
+    -2/(7*sqrt(pi))
+    >>> real_gaunt(10,10,20,-9,-9,0).n(64)
+    -0.00002480019791932209313156167...
+
+    It is an error to use non-integer values for `l` and `m`::
+
+        real_gaunt(2.8,0.5,1.3,0,0,0)
+        Traceback (most recent call last):
+        ...
+        ValueError: l values must be integer
+        real_gaunt(2,2,4,0.7,1,-3.4)
+        Traceback (most recent call last):
+        ...
+        ValueError: m values must be integer
+
+    Notes
+    =====
+
+    The real Gaunt coefficient inherits from the standard Gaunt coefficient, 
+    the invariance under any permutation of the pairs `(l_i, m_i)` and the 
+    requirement that the sum of the `l_i` be even to yield a non-zero value. 
+    It also obeys the following symmetry rules:
+
+    - zero for `l_1`, `l_2`, `l_3` not fulfiling the condition 
+    `l_1 \in \{l_{\text{max}}, l_{\text{max}}-2, \ldots, l_{\text{min}}\}`,
+    where `l_{\text{max}} = l_2+l_3`, 
+    
+    ..math::
+    
+        \begin{aligned}
+        l_{\text{min}} = \begin{cases} \kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3) & \text{if}\, 
+        \kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3) + l_{\text{max}}\, \text{is even} \\ 
+        \kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3)+1 & \text{if}\, \kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3) +
+        l_{\text{max}}\, \text{is odd}\end{cases}
+        \end{aligned}
+        
+    and `\kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3) = \max{\big(|l_2-l_3|, \min{\big(|m_2+m_3|, 
+    |m_2-m_3|\big)}\big)}`
+      
+    - zero for an odd number of negative `m_i`
+
+    Algorithms
+    ==========
+
+    This function uses the algorithms of [Homeier96]_ and [Rasch03]_ to
+    calculate the value of the real Gaunt coefficient exactly. Note that
+    the formula used in [Rasch03]_ contains alternating sums over large
+    factorials and is therefore unsuitable for finite precision arithmetic
+    and only useful for a computer algebra system [Rasch03]_. However, this
+    function can in principle use any algorithm that computes the Gaunt
+    coefficient, so it is suitable for finite precision arithmetic in so far 
+    as the algorithm which computes the Gaunt coefficient is.
+
+    """
+    if int(l_1) != l_1 or int(l_2) != l_2 or int(l_3) != l_3:
+        raise ValueError("l values must be integer")
+    if int(m_1) != m_1 or int(m_2) != m_2 or int(m_3) != m_3:

Typical calculations do not use that high an l value, but I have seen calculations where the l values reached up to 100.

> We just need enough tests to ensure that if anyone modifies the algorithm that we get catch errors. My first pass through, I had a different sign. So maybe just generating all results over a small range of integers would work:

I have tested the algorithm for the range `0 < l_1, l_2, l_3 < 10` and have generated a txt file containing all non-zero values. I can upload it somewhere in the repository so that it can be referred to in the event of modifications. 

[Non-Zero Real Gaunt Coefficients for l1, l2, l3 Between 0 and 10.txt](https://github.com/sympy/sympy/files/9541961/Non-Zero.Real.Gaunt.Coefficients.for.l1.l2.l3.Between.0.and.10.txt)

I have not found any pull requests related to my proposed changes.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, and the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation have been added throughout the entire file. No other code was modified or deleted.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Big thanks to Christopher Smith for suggesting a number of modifications that have made the program more concise and reduced the runtime significantly.

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

The sum `2+2+4` is even but exchange of 2 and -1 makes it odd. What am I misunderstanding?

The real Gaunt coefficient is the integral of 3 real spherical harmonics, so the first three real_gaunt function arguments refer to the l values of the three real spherical harmonics and the next three, to their corresponding m values. The l values are restricted to be natural numbers while the m values can be any integer between `[-l, l]`. The result is then invariant to permutations of the pair `(l_i, m_i)`, meaning that you get the same answer if you swap both an l and corresponding m value simultaneously, but not separately. Does that make sense? It doesn't speed up the calculation since the calculation is not recursive and it is standard-sympy to let the user choose to evaluate explicitly rather than to have the function do so via `prec` option` (unless the result is typically unwieldy in explicit for).

I set it up that way to mimic the original `gaunt` function in this module as much as possible, but I take your point.

> Here is a suggestion for the short circuit calcs:
> 
> ```python
>     # check for quick exits
>     if sum(1 for i in (m_1, m_2, m_3) if i < 0) % 2:
>         return S.Zero  # odd number of negative m
>     if (l_1 + l_2 + l_3) % 2:
>         return S.Zero  # sum of l is odd
>     kappa = lambda a, b, c, d: max(abs(a - b),min(abs(c + d),abs(c - d)))
>     lmax = l_2 + l_3
>     lmin = kappa(l_2, l_3, m_2, m_3)
>     if (lmin + lmax)%2:
>         lmin += 1
>     if lmin not in range(lmax, lmin - 2, -2):
>         return S.Zero
>     # full calc
>     kron_del = lambda i, j: 1 if i == j else 0
>     s = lambda e: -1 if e % 2 else 1
>     A = lambda a, b: (-kron_del(a, b)*s(a - b) + kron_del(a, -b)*
>                       s(b)) if b < 0 else 0
>     B = lambda a, b: (kron_del(a, b) + kron_del(a, -b)*s(a)) if b > 0 else 0
>     C = lambda a, b: kron_del(abs(a), abs(b))*(kron_del(a, 0)*kron_del(b, 0) +
>                                           (B(a, b) + I*A(a, b))/sqrt(2))
>     ugnt = 0
>     for i in range(-l_1, l_1+1):
>         U1 = C(i, m_1)
>         for j in range(-l_2, l_2+1):
>             U2 = C(j, m_2)
>             U3 = C(-i - j, m_3)
>             ugnt = ugnt + re(U1*U2*U3)*gaunt(l_1, l_2, l_3, i, j, -i - j)

Almost everything looks good to me. The only things missing are the line to check for integer arguments that you suggested earlier, and I think we can just assign the value of the kappa function with arguments subbed in directly to `lmin` because it is only used in that one instance.

I have not found any pull requests related to my proposed changes.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, along with the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation scattered throughout the wigner.py file. A test function titled test_realgaunt was added to the testing file within the physics module as well. Lastly, the test functions for the clebsch-gordon function were all condensed down to one.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Very big thanks to Christopher Smith for suggesting a number of modifications that have made the program more concise and reduced the runtime significantly.

#### References to other Issues or PRs
<!-- If this pull request fixes an issue, write "Fixes #NNNN" in that exact
format, e.g. "Fixes #1234" (see
https://tinyurl.com/auto-closing for more information). Also, please
write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is
open. -->
I have not found any pull requests related to my proposed changes.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, along with the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation scattered throughout the wigner.py file. A test function titled test_realgaunt was added to the testing file within the physics module as well. Lastly, the test functions for the clebsch-gordon function were all condensed down to one.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Very big thanks to Christopher Smith for suggesting a number of modifications that have made the program more concise and reduced the runtime significantly.

  * Added `real_gaunt` function to compute the integral of 3 real spherical harmonics.

I have not found any pull requests related to my proposed changes.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, along with the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation scattered throughout the wigner.py file. A test function titled test_realgaunt was added to the testing file within the physics module as well. Lastly, the test functions for the clebsch-gordon function were all condensed down to one.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Very big thanks to Christopher Smith for suggesting a number of modifications that have made the program more concise and reduced the runtime significantly.

#### Release Notes

<!-- Write the release notes for this release below between the BEGIN and END
statements. The basic format is a bulleted list with the name of the subpackage
and the release note for this PR. For example:

* functions
  * Fixed a bug with log of integers.

or if no release note(s) should be included use:

NO ENTRY

See https://github.com/sympy/sympy/wiki/Writing-Release-Notes for more
information on how to write release notes. The bot will check your release
notes automatically to see if they are formatted correctly. -->

<!-- BEGIN RELEASE NOTES -->

* physics.wigner
  * Added `real_gaunt` function to compute the integral of 3 real spherical harmonics. 

<!-- END RELEASE NOTES -->

I have not found any pull requests related to my proposed changes.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, along with the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation scattered throughout the wigner.py file. A test function titled test_realgaunt was added to the testing file within the physics module as well. Lastly, the test functions for the clebsch-gordon function were all condensed down to one.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Very big thanks to Christopher Smith for suggesting a number of modifications that have made the program more concise and reduced the runtime significantly.

I have coded a symbolic algorithm to compute the integral of three real spherical harmonics based on the outline presented in Homeier and Steinborn (1996). The appropriate import lines, references, and documentation have also been added throughout the entire file.

#### References to other Issues or PRs
<!-- If this pull request fixes an issue, write "Fixes #NNNN" in that exact
format, e.g. "Fixes #1234" (see
https://tinyurl.com/auto-closing for more information). Also, please
write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is

I proposed these changes with the help of @smichr in pull request #24035, but I am new to GitHub and didnt merge the changes properly so I am opening a new pull request.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, along with the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation scattered throughout the wigner.py file. A test function titled test_realgaunt was added to the testing file within the physics module as well. Lastly, the test functions for the clebsch-gordon function were all condensed down to one.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Very big thanks to Christopher Smith for suggesting a number of modifications that have made the program more concise and reduced the runtime significantly.

Your title above should be a short description of what
was changed. Do not include the issue number in the title. -->

I have coded a symbolic algorithm to compute the integral of three real spherical harmonics based on the outline presented in Homeier and Steinborn (1996). The appropriate import lines, references, and documentation have also been added throughout the entire file.

#### References to other Issues or PRs
<!-- If this pull request fixes an issue, write "Fixes #NNNN" in that exact
format, e.g. "Fixes #1234" (see
https://tinyurl.com/auto-closing for more information). Also, please
write a comment on that issue linking back to this pull request once it is
open. -->

I proposed these changes with the help of @smichr in pull request #24035, but I am new to GitHub and didnt merge the changes properly so I am opening a new pull request.

#### Brief description of what is fixed or changed

An algorithm to compute the real Gaunt coefficient has been added as a function titled real_gaunt, along with the appropriate import lines, references, and documentation scattered throughout the wigner.py file. A test function titled test_realgaunt was added to the testing file within the physics module as well. Lastly, the test functions for the clebsch-gordon function were all condensed down to one.

#### Other comments

The algorithm uses the already included Gaunt algorithm, but I can find another if this is problematic (due to copyright issues perhaps). Please also let me know if anyone finds any bugs or other conflicting lines.

EDIT: Very big thanks to Christopher Smith for suggesting a number of modifications that have made the program more concise and reduced the runtime significantly.
